key_insight: The paper turns persistent UAS surveillance into a provably correct decentralized control problem by combining cyclic team reconfiguration, bounded LTL mission logic, and local communication.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical blueprint for swarm surveillance systems that must maintain coverage and formation performance under energy and communication limits while preserving formal guarantees.
method: Partition the swarm into stationary anchors and mobile workers with cyclic replacement modes.
Use a bounded LTL hierarchy to encode reference consistency, finite-time reachability, tracking, and coverage requirements.
Synthesize new worker reference configurations by minimizing KL divergence, which also fixes a deterministic mode-dependent communication topology.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical simulations showing cyclic reconfiguration, decentralized topology synthesis, finite-time formation convergence, and certified persistent surveillance coverage.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate model assumptions and mode-dependent coordination rules, so real-world deployment may be sensitive to disturbances, sensing errors, or communication dropouts.
paper_title: Distributed Continuous Aerial Surveillance by UAS Swarms Under Formal Mission Specifications
